name: Namespace Management
description: Create, organize, and manage Kubernetes namespaces with resource quotas and limit ranges for multi-tenant clusters.

steps.title: List all namespaces with status
steps.description: View all namespaces and their current status.

steps.title: Create a namespace with labels
steps.description: Create a new namespace and label it for organization.
steps.command: kubectl create namespace staging && kubectl label namespace staging environment=staging team=platform

steps.title: Set a resource quota on a namespace
steps.description: Limit the total resources that can be consumed in a namespace.
steps.tip: Resource quotas prevent a single team from consuming all cluster resources.

steps.title: View resource usage in a namespace
steps.description: Check current resource consumption against quotas.

steps.title: Set the default namespace for kubectl
steps.description: Change the default namespace so you do not need to specify -n on every command.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Namespace Management
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== All Namespaces ==="
kubectl get namespaces

echo ""
echo "=== Resource Quotas ==="
for ns in $(kubectl get namespaces -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'); do
  quota=$(kubectl describe quota -n "$ns" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$quota" ]; then
    echo "--- $ns ---"
    echo "$quota" | grep -E 'Name:|Used|Hard' | head -5
    echo ""
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Current Context Namespace ==="
kubectl config view --minify -o jsonpath='{.contexts[0].context.namespace}' && echo "" || echo "default"

faq.question: Can I move resources between namespaces?
faq.answer: No, Kubernetes does not support moving resources between namespaces. You need to delete and recreate the resource in the new namespace.

faq.question: What is a LimitRange vs ResourceQuota?
faq.answer: ResourceQuota sets total limits for the entire namespace. LimitRange sets default and maximum limits per individual pod or container. Use both together for comprehensive resource management.
